title: 10 best herbal tinctures for runny nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The bioactive compounds in purple coneflower, such as echinacoside and caffeic acid, work to combat inflammation and relieve congestion, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discharge. Additionally, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of purple coneflower help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose, providing a natural solution for sinus relief and respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Purple coneflower tincture contains compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antihistamine activity, helping to alleviate nasal congestion and runny nose by reducing histamine release.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, which may contribute to a runny nose.

Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower tincture contains compounds like ursolic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens, potentially helping to combat infections that may cause a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating the symptoms of a runny nose.
Cichoric Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and fluid production in the nasal mucosa, leading to relief from a runny nose.

Alkylamides: These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the body's immune response and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to nasal congestion and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help alleviate sinusitis and congestion.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Flowers: They are a key component in many herbal remedies, as they have antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the fall when the plant is dormant and the flowers have faded.
Dry the roots and flowers in a single layer on paper bags or a drying rack for 1-2 weeks.
Chop 1 part of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka.
Steep the chopped plant material in the vodka for 2-6 weeks in a dark glass jar with a lid, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with runny nose because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The tannins and flavonoids present in licorice root also work to constrict blood vessels, reducing excess mucus production and sinus pressure. Additionally, licorice root has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out congestion, providing quick relief from a runny nose. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for those who suffer from allergies or colds.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating a runny nose.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice root help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental pollutants, which can contribute to a runny nose.
Expectorant: Licorice root has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and runniness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpene glycosides exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin, a flavonoid present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe nasal passages and reduce mucus production.
Liquiritin: A flavanone glycoside, liquiritin has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce nasal discharge associated with runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: As they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties that can help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Leaves: As they are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe a runny nose.
Barks: As they contain a mixture of flavonoids and saponins, similar to the leaves, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 kg of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Clean and dry the roots completely to prevent any contamination or moisture from affecting tincture quality.
Chop 500g of the dried roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for solvent absorption.
Steep the chopped roots in 2 liters of 40% ethanol solvent for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any solids and achieve a clear liquid consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with runny nose because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce nasal congestion and swelling, which can contribute to a runny nose. Additionally, dandelion's antihistaminic properties can help to alleviate sinus pressure and itching, providing relief from allergy-related symptoms. By reducing inflammation and alleviating sinus pressure, herbal dandelion tinctures can help to dry up a runny nose and promote clearer breathing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving excess mucus production and Runny nose symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate runny nose symptoms such as itchiness and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: Dandelion's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Taraxasterol acetate: This compound has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine activities, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and runny nose associated with allergies.
Chlorogenic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nasal congestion.
Roots: The roots are used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their natural antihistamine properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies that often cause a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in early spring or late autumn when they are at their most potent.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine 100 grams of chopped taraxacum officinale with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Leafy spurge tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating excessive mucus production. As a result, leafy spurge tincture can help to dry up a runny nose and provide fast relief from sinus pressure and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the nasal membranes, promoting healthy sinus function.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Leafy spurge tincture helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce nasal secretions, providing quick relief from excessive mucus production and runny noses.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and a decrease in runny nose symptoms.
Expectorant action: Leafy spurge contains expectorant compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Alkylphenones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose.

Terpenoids (including euphorbol and euphorbin): These bioactive compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce nasal discharge associated with runny nose.

Phenolic acids (including ferulic acid): As potent antioxidants, these compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the roots and stems of the plant in late summer or early fall when the sap is dry.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part of the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
remedy_parts: Eucalyptus leaves: Known for their decongestant properties, which help to relieve congestion and runny nose.
Ginger roots: Used in traditional medicine to treat colds and flu, which can cause runny nose.
Peppermint leaves: Known for their decongestant and antihistamine properties, which help to relieve congestion and runny nose.

The flavonoids present in yarrow also have astringent effects, constricting blood vessels and reducing excess mucus production. Additionally, yarrow's antibacterial properties help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, which can alleviate excessive mucus production and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in yarrow tincture, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the nasal mucosa, further reducing congestion and runny nose symptoms.

Phenolic Acids (specifically, chlorogenic acid and 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate runny nose symptoms.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly camphor, which helps to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Achillea millefolium contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have decongestant properties that help to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Clean the harvested plant material with water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Chop the clean achillea millefolium plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine one part of the chopped plant material with two parts of 80% ethanol in a glass container.
Store the container in a cool dark place for four weeks to allow the tincture to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with runny nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus, allowing the body to naturally expel it. Additionally, elder tincture's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause runny noses, such as the common cold or sinusitis. remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins, help combat viruses that cause respiratory infections, reducing congestion and runny nose symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids, reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion, sinus pressure, and runny nose discomfort.
Mucolytic properties: Elder's mucolytic activity breaks down mucus, allowing it to be easily cleared from the body and relieving congestion, postnasal drip, and runny nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.

Salicin: A glycoside derived from salicylic acid, salicin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and promote the clearance of excess mucus, alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are used to make tinctures for runny nose due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Sambucus nigra fruits are used to make tinctures for runny nose due to their antiviral and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate congestion.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used to make tinctures for runny nose due to their decongestant properties that help relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Clean the sambucus nigra plant material by gently rinsing it with cold water to remove any debris.
Steep 250 grams of sambucus nigra plant material in 1 liter of 35% ethanol for 2 weeks to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth to remove the plant material and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. Phenolic acids: As potent antioxidants, phenolic acids can help combat inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal mucosa, contributing to the reduction of runny nose.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the primary part used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are also used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in relieving a runny nose.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used due to their content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them completely to preserve their properties for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion and tincture making.
Combine 1 part calendula flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day to facilitate the extraction process for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.

The herb's essential oils, such as citral and limonene, have a decongestant effect that can alleviate excess mucus production and sinus pressure, making it an effective remedy for relieving a runny nose. remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: Verbascoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce nasal inflammation and congestion, alleviating runny nose symptoms.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils and flavonoids that help in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with runny nose.

Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried plant tops in early morning after dew has evaporated when flowers are in bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material at 70 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Combine 1 part of dried plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discard the plant material.

The tannins present in the herb have a natural decongestant effect, reducing excess mucus production and swelling in the nasal passages. Additionally, the antiviral compounds in Tasmanian blue gum work to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the congestion, helping to clear out the sinuses and restore normal breathing patterns. remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Eucalyptus globulus for making tinctures due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and expectorant.
Buds: Buds are used for tinctures because they contain a higher concentration of eucalyptol than mature leaves, making them more potent for relieving respiratory issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 1 cup of eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks or until brittle.
Combine 1 cup of the dried plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with runny nose because it contains berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that targets the root cause of respiratory issues. When taken internally, berberine reduces inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating excessive mucus production and discharge. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing welcome relief from sinus pressure and congestion. remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.

Berbamine: Berbamine, another important compound in goldenseal, exhibits expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and rhinorrhea (runny nose).

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in goldenseal tincture, including berberine and berbamine, also has a direct effect on the nasal mucosa, reducing inflammation and constriction of blood vessels, which helps to ease sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing nasal congestion and fighting off infections.
Barks: The barks of the goldenseal plant are rich in alkaloids like berberine and cantharidin, which help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh or dried goldenseal roots hydrastis canadensis and cut into small pieces.
Combine the root pieces with 4 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar leaving one inch headspace.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily ensuring all pieces are saturated.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles filled to the top and store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried equisetum arvense herb at the recommended dosage of 100-200 grams.
Cleanly chop the harvested equisetum arvense into smaller pieces and place them in a glass jar.
Fill the glass jar with vodka or everclear at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts liquid covering the plant material completely.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks allowing it to steep and extract its active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids after preparation time.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers immediately to prevent contamination.
Combine one part dried rosa rugosa flowers with two parts vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing for gentle extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The herb's expectorant compounds help to thin and loosen excess mucus, allowing for easier drainage and relief from congestion. Additionally, comfrey's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties may help to reduce nasal swelling and combat underlying infections that can contribute to chronic runny nose. As a result, using an herbal comfrey tincture can provide natural and effective relief from persistent nasal discharge.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Comfrey's tannins and flavonoids help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and excess mucus.
Expectorant properties: The mucilages present in comfrey help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing the need for frequent blowing of the nose.
Antioxidant activity: Comfrey's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can exacerbate a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect mucous membranes, making it effective for relieving congestion and runny noses.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale and wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine one part chopped leaves with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for at least six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to thin and loosen mucus in the nasal passages. The bioactive compounds found in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work to ease congestion and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and breathe more freely. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose caused by allergies, colds, or sinus infections.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can help alleviate nasal congestion, itchiness, and runniness.
Antihistamine property: Fennel's antihistamine effects can help counteract histamine release, a key factor in allergic reactions that can cause a runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a popular choice for treating runny nose as they contain a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, including runny nose, due to their ability to thin mucus and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at peak potency for optimal effectiveness.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 24-48 hours.
Measure out 20% of dried foeniculum vulgare by weight and combine with 80% high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling the tincture for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with runny nose because its natural antihistamine properties help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The tincture's ability to calm and soothe the mucous membranes also aids in reducing excess mucus production, resulting in a clearer and more manageable breathing experience. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help to combat any underlying inflammation that may be contributing to the runny nose, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal swelling and congestion, making it easier to breathe.

Antimicrobial: The essential oils present in English lavender tincture, such as lavendin and camphor, exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may be causing the runny nose, helping to prevent infections and promote healing.

Calming and Sedative: Lavender's sedative properties can help relax the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose, as well as promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation in individuals who are suffering from respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm nasal passages, reducing congestion and runniness.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease nasal irritation, making them effective for addressing runny noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom for optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with two parts vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the jar daily for 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids after preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The tannins present in sage bind to excess mucus, reducing congestion and thinning the discharge to make it easier to expel. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial agents combat underlying infections that can cause prolonged runny noses. By addressing these root causes, sage tinctures provide effective relief from runny nose symptoms, allowing for clearer breathing and improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and runniness.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in sage tincture help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, reducing the severity of a runny nose.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, which can help alleviate congestion and runniness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Leaves: Sage leaves are utilized due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing excessive mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning for optimal potency.
Weigh 50 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and add them to a clean glass jar.
Pour 100% pure ethanol over the dried herb, ensuring it is completely saturated and then seal the jar tightly.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with dropper lids, label and store in a cool dark place for future use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for runny nose are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that can effectively alleviate congestion, sinus pressure, and excess mucus production. These natural remedies help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy drainage. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with runny nose include Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Peppermint, which can be taken orally or added to warm tea for instant relief. By using these tinctures, people with chronic sinus issues or seasonal allergies can breathe easier, sleep better, and enjoy improved overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal tinctures are various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, congestion, and coughs. Herbal tinctures containing herbs like echinacea, goldenseal, and yarrow are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sinus infections, and allergies. They help reduce inflammation, open airways, and stimulate the immune system, providing relief from discomforting runny noses, sneezing, and congestion.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with runny nose is Echinacea and Goldenseal. Echinacea, rich in antioxidants, supports the immune system and reduces inflammation. Goldenseal, with its antimicrobial properties, helps combat infections and congestion. Additionally, incorporating a small amount of Eucalyptus tincture can help loosen mucus and clear airways. A balanced blend of these three tinctures can provide relief from runny nose, congestion, and sinus pressure, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
